steps.action: Select A/G or A/A master mode
steps.detail: Press the A/G or A/A master mode button on the instrument panel to set the appropriate combat mode before arming.

steps.action: Confirm weapon selection on SMS DDI
steps.detail: Verify the correct store is selected on the Stores Management System DDI page and the correct station is active.

steps.action: Set Master Arm switch to ARM
steps.detail: Lift the guard and move the Master Arm switch to ARM on the Master Arm panel (instrument panel, left side).

steps.action: Set Laser Arm switch to ARM (LGB/guided weapons only)
steps.detail: For laser-guided weapons, also set the Laser Arm switch to ARM. This is separate from Master Arm and must be set independently.

steps.action: Confirm MASTER ARM caution clears on DDI
steps.detail: The MASTER ARM advisory should appear on the HUD and DDI when armed. Verify no SAFE indication remains.

steps.action: Display TGP page on a DDI
steps.detail: Press OSB next to FLIR/TPOD on the sensor select page, or select it from the DDI menu. The TGP page should show the FLIR video feed.

steps.action: Set TGP to STBY then OPR
steps.detail: Allow the pod to warm up in STBY (approximately 60 seconds cold), then select OPR. Video feed will appear once operational.

steps.action: Select FLIR polarity and FOV
steps.detail: Choose WHITE HOT or BLACK HOT polarity via OSB. Select wide FOV (WFOV) initially to find the target, then narrow (MFOV/NFOV) to designate.

steps.action: Slew crosshairs to target area
steps.detail: Use the TDC (Throttle Designator Controller) to slew the TGP crosshairs over the target. In A/G mode the TGP is SOI when selected via TDC depress.

steps.action: TDC depress to lock and track
steps.detail: Depress the TDC to command the TGP into POINT TRACK or AREA TRACK mode. The pod will slave to the designated point. Verify TRACK indication appears on the DDI.

steps.action: Verify TRACK mode and laser ready
steps.detail: Confirm the TGP DDI shows TRACK (not SRCH). The laser will only fire when the pod is in TRACK mode. Laser Arm must be ARM for lasing.

steps.action: Complete TGP designation sequence
steps.detail: Designate the target with the TGP before setting up for the drop. TGP must be in TRACK mode. Reference tgp_designate_sequence.

steps.action: Select GBU-12 on SMS DDI
steps.detail: Navigate to the SMS DDI page and select the GBU-12 station. Confirm GBU-12 appears as the selected weapon.

steps.action: Set A/G master mode
steps.detail: Confirm A/G master mode is active. The HUD will display the A/G steering cue and release envelope.

steps.action: Set Master Arm to ARM, Laser Arm to ARM
steps.detail: Both Master Arm and Laser Arm must be ARM. Laser Arm is on the same Master Arm panel.

steps.action: Fly the release cue — confirm inside release envelope
steps.detail: Maneuver to place the HUD steering dot inside the release envelope diamond. Minimum release altitude is 1500 ft AGL for fuze arming. Optimal: 10,000–15,000 ft AGL.

steps.action: Pickle (press and hold weapon release)
steps.detail: Press and hold the weapons release (pickle) button. The GBU-12 will release when the system computes optimal release. Do not break lock after release.

steps.action: Maintain TGP track until impact
steps.detail: Keep the TGP crosshair on the target until bomb impact. The laser fires automatically based on computed time-of-flight. Do not maneuver aggressively — keep the target in the TGP FOV.

steps.action: Select A/A master mode
steps.detail: Press the A/A master mode button. Radar will automatically enter air-to-air search mode (RWS by default).

steps.action: Power radar — verify not in STBY or OFF
steps.detail: Confirm radar is in RWS, TWS, or another operating mode. Radar must be emitting to support AMRAAM active guidance until pitbull.

steps.action: Select AIM-120C on SMS
steps.detail: Verify AIM-120C is the selected A/A weapon on the SMS page. The HUD will show the AMRAAM steering circle.

steps.action: Set Master Arm to ARM
steps.detail: Set the Master Arm switch to ARM. No separate laser arm is required for AMRAAM.

steps.action: Acquire target — STT lock or TWS track
steps.detail: For a single-target BVR shot, TDC depress on a RWS contact to command STT. Alternatively, use TWS for multi-target. STT provides continuous uplink; TWS allows pitbull shot.

steps.action: Confirm in-range — shoot when inside Rmax
steps.detail: The HUD steering cue and DLZ (Dynamic Launch Zone) indicate launch range. Shoot inside the active range ring. Call FOX THREE on radio.

steps.action: Maintain radar contact until pitbull
steps.detail: The AIM-120 relies on radar uplink until its onboard seeker goes active (pitbull). Maintain STT or TWS track. After pitbull you may crank or notch; the missile guides autonomously.
